Milestone M3: graph input and output experiments

Reframed by the SGM redirection (nodes 53-54): the graph experiments become the SGM architecture decision, serialized versus graph-native, rather than a context-format comparison. Once binding clears its gate on flat text, run the existing graph-context pipeline to test whether graph-structured context binds referents better than plain text at matched token budgets and fixed parameters, the project's core research payload.

M3 is the graph input/output study, gated strictly on the binding gate (node 45): graph-versus-flat comparisons are uninterpretable while the flat model cannot bind referents from plain context, because the graph hypothesis is precisely that structured context binds better, so a non-zero flat baseline is required to measure against. The graph stages (graph_transform through graph_evaluate) already exist in the pipeline. Measurement holds parameters fixed and compares graph-structured context against plain-text context over the same worlds, scoring in-context binding (node 33) and judged coherence. It follows M1 and M2 (nodes 48, 49) because it needs both coherent data and a model with headroom above the binding floor.

This is the experiment the project exists to run (goal G2, node 2): whether presenting context as an explicit relational graph, rather than as prose the model must parse, lets a small from-scratch model hold and combine facts it otherwise loses. The gate in node 45 makes the ordering non-negotiable: a model that fails to retrieve a fact stated three sentences earlier fails both the graph and the flat condition for reasons unrelated to context structure, so the comparison would measure noise. M1 (node 48) opens that gate by making binding non-zero through data coherence, and M2 (node 49) widens the margin so the comparison has headroom. The design keeps parameters and token budget fixed and varies only the context representation over shared worlds from the same world-state machinery (node 36), scoring with the judge-free binding probe (node 33) as the primary instrument and coherence as corroboration. Extensions that become worthwhile only here include multi-hop binding questions that combine two stated facts, longer contexts, and larger context budgets, all already anticipated as binding-probe extensions (node 33). The existing graph pipeline is run unchanged; this milestone records when and how to run it, not a change to it. Reframed (nodes 53-55): the canonical episode graph is the SIM's native log, so graph context stops being an experimental alternative and becomes the controller's world representation. M3 is now the architecture decision itself: SGM v0 (the decoder over the canonical serialization) versus a graph-native successor, compared at matched token budgets and parameters on the SIM evaluation axes.
